raw_consumer_statement:
I signed up for a free trial with FuboTV in early 2025 and believed I had canceled before the trial ended. The service was never used after the trial period, yet my American Express card was billed for about a year, totaling roughly {$1000.00}. When I discovered the charges, I contacted XXXX, which issued a partial refund for XXXX month but refused to refund the remaining {$680.00}. 

I filed a dispute with American Express on XX/XX/year>, but they closed it immediately, stating they could not investigate because the subscription was created online and that I should contact the merchant directly. I later spoke with an AmEx dispute specialist who confirmed they could not reopen the case for the same reason.

I believe these charges qualify as a billing dispute under the Fair Credit Billing Act since I did not receive or use the service for which I was billed. I am asking American Express to review this dispute properly and issue a credit for the remaining unused charges.
